mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals rapid labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives equity valuations higher over the next trading month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5 percent over any rolling 12-month period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production increase exceeding 1.0 percent demonstrates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings revisions and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns average below zero when the trigger activates, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP above 2.0 percent per annum reflects sustained economic momentum, which systematically lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent consistently coincides with negative or flat 60-day forward equity returns across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading that exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year triggers fears of premature monetary tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC remains above 0.05 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly payroll growth exceeding 500,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market tightening, which accelerates wage-driven consumption and lifts short-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate statistically significant positive alpha (p > 0.05) over any 24-month rolling window, or the interaction coefficient flips sign against the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rising ratio of industrial production to real GDP growth indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, which systematically drags down S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO/GDPC1 ratio rises for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The margin compression mechanism is rejected if the ratio increase consistently coincides with positive equity returns, or the forward return differential between high and low ratio regimes falls below 1.0 percent.
